# พักใจ (Phak Jai) — Thailand's Free Meditation Retreat Directory

> พักใจ is a non-profit directory of 50+ verified free meditation centers across 23 provinces in Thailand. Updated April 2026. All data sourced directly from each center's official website.

## Quick Facts
- **Total listings**: 50+ meditation centers
- **Provinces covered**: 23 (including Bangkok, Chiang Mai, Nakhon Ratchasima, Surat Thani, Mae Hong Son)
- **Cost**: All listings are free or donation-based
- **Languages**: Thai (primary), English (secondary)
- **Founded**: 2025
- **Status**: Non-profit
- **URL**: https://pakjai.co

## What is พักใจ (Phak Jai)?
พักใจ is a curated, practitioner-verified directory that connects people — especially young Thai adults experiencing stress, burnout, or mental health challenges — with free meditation retreats across Thailand. The name "พักใจ" means "rest the mind" in Thai.

## Key Features
- **Temple Directory** (https://pakjai.co/): Browse all centers with filters for location, duration (day trip or overnight), and facility type
- **Recommendation Quiz** (https://pakjai.co/recommendation.html): Answer 4 questions to find the right center for you
- **Articles** (https://pakjai.co/articles/): Guides for beginners, Bangkok meditation spots, and English-friendly centers

## Types of Meditation Available
- **วิปัสสนา (Vipassana)**: Insight meditation, typically 3-10 day courses. Observing body and mind systematically.
- **สมถะ (Samatha)**: Concentration meditation, calming the mind to a single point. Can start with 15 minutes/day.
- **เจริญสติ (Mindfulness)**: Awareness practice, suitable for daily life application.
- **อานาปานสติ (Anapanasati)**: Breath meditation, accessible for complete beginners.

## Popular Free Meditation Centers in Thailand

### Bangkok (กรุงเทพฯ)
- **วัดมหาธาตุยุวราชรังสฤษฎิ์** — Daily Vipassana, International Meditation Center, near Tha Phra Chan
- **เสถียรธรรมสถาน** — 3-day programs (Fri-Sun), Anapanasati focus, Ram Intra area
- **สวนโมกข์กรุงเทพ** — Mindfulness activities, Chatuchak area
- **วัดยานนาวา** — 3-day and 7-day courses, Sathorn area near BTS

### Chiang Mai (เชียงใหม่)
- **วัดร่ำเปิง (ตโปทาราม)** — 10-26 day Vipassana, structured curriculum
- **วัดอุโมงค์** — Flexible practice, forest temple setting
- **Doi Suthep Vipassana Center** — English-friendly programs

### Other Provinces
- **สวนโมกขพลาราม** (Surat Thani) — Suan Mokkh International, monthly 10-day silent retreats (1st of each month)
- **วัดป่าตามวา** (Mae Hong Son) — Wat Pa Tam Wua, free 3-10 day stays, English instruction, vegan meals
- **ศูนย์วิปัสสนาธรรมกาญจนา** (Kanchanaburi) — Goenka tradition, 10-day courses

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Q: Are meditation retreats in Thailand really free?
A: Yes. Most traditional Buddhist meditation centers in Thailand operate on a dana (donation) basis. Accommodation, meals, and instruction are provided free of charge. Donations are welcome but never required.

### Q: Do I need meditation experience?
A: No. Many centers welcome complete beginners. พักใจ's directory lets you filter for beginner-friendly centers. Day programs (ปฏิบัติธรรม 1 วัน) are ideal for first-timers.

### Q: Can foreigners attend?
A: Yes. Many centers accept non-Thai speakers. Centers like Wat Mahathat International Center, Suan Mokkh IDH, and Wat Pa Tam Wua offer English instruction. See our English-friendly guide: https://pakjai.co/articles/5-meditation-places-for-foreign-friends.html

### Q: What should I bring?
A: White or modest clothing, personal toiletries, and an open mind. Most centers provide sleeping mats and meals. Mobile phones are typically not allowed during practice periods.

### Q: How long are retreats?
A: Options range from 1-day programs (ไปเช้า-เย็นกลับ) to 26-day intensive courses. Common durations: 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 10 days.

- Report data corrections: https://pakjai.co/report.html
- Recommend a new center: https://pakjai.co/recommend.html
- Project: สืบธรรม (Sueptham) — non-profit initiative
